description: Autoscaling a GPU-bound server on CPU utilization, so it never scales while the accelerator and queue saturate.
body: Failure mode: autoscaling is keyed on CPU, but the workload is GPU-bound, so CPU stays low while the accelerator saturates and the request queue grows — and the scaler never triggers. Detection signals: queue depth and latency climb while CPU-based scaling stays flat; replicas do not increase under load; the GPU is saturated but CPU is idle. Do not scale GPU workloads on CPU. Safer fallback: scale on queue depth, batch latency, or accelerator utilization, set the target below saturation, and account for replica warm-up time.
